On the evening of April 18, 1775, Provincial leaders in Boston learn that General Gage is sending 700 British Regulars to raid a stockpile of military supplies in Concord. Paul Revere arranges for two lanterns to be lit in the belfry of North Church, signaling that the Regulars are heading out by water. One of the two used as a signal, April 18, 1775. origin_prompt: Create a comprehensive Battle of Lexington report that weaves together a concise historical narrative of the battle and the famous "shot heard ’round the world" with a terrain elevation map of the Lexington area showing British and colonial troop positions. The report should also feature annotated map images that highlight key maneuvers and engagement zones, a timeline chart that visualizes the sequence of events with timestamps, and a polished PDF document that integrates the narrative, all visualizations, and proper source citations. The work process involves locating reputable historical maps, elevation data, and primary source excerpts about the battle, producing a contour elevation map and a timeline chart, calculating approximate distances between reported troop positions, creating a base historical map illustration and overlaying bounding boxes and labels for British and colonial units, movement arrows, and key locations, and finally assembling the textual analysis, visual assets, distance calculations, and citations into a formatted PDF. The deliverable is the final PDF file accompanied by a brief summary of the data sources and calculations performed.

full_tree.requirements: Produce a complete PDF battle report that integrates narrative, visualizations, calculations, and citations

sub_tasks.requirements: A concise historical narrative of the Battle of Lexington, including the "shot heard ’round the world", is written and embedded in the PDF
A terrain elevation contour map of Lexington showing British and colonial troop positions is generated and embedded in the PDF
Annotated map images that highlight key maneuvers, unit bounding boxes, and movement arrows are created and included in the PDF
A timeline chart visualizing the sequence of events with timestamps is produced and added to the PDF
A bibliography with properly formatted source citations is compiled and appended to the PDF

full_tree.requirements: Calculate and document spatial relationships between British and colonial troop positions

sub_tasks.requirements: Approximate distances between reported British and colonial positions are computed and presented in a table within the PDF

full_tree.requirements: Validate historical accuracy and logical coherence of the report

sub_tasks.requirements: All factual statements, dates, and locations are cross‑checked against reputable primary and secondary sources and a fact‑check summary is included in the PDF
The narrative clearly articulates causality between actions, motivations, and outcomes, and this logical flow is verified by a coherence review
Accurate historical terminology and correct spellings of names, places, and units are confirmed throughout the document
